prevalence.value: 10-15% lifetime in women (symptomatic UTI; underdiagnosis likely)

burden.mortalityRate: Low overall; higher in complicated UTI and sepsis

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms with urinalysis; culture for complicated/recurrent cases; resistance-aware empiric therapy is important

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; resistance and limited culture access affect outcomes

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis common; culture access often limited outside urban centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Nitrofurantoin/fosfomycin where available; TMP-SMX and fluoroquinolones impacted by resistance; pregnancy-specific regimens and referral for pyelonephritis
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High antimicrobial resistance and out-of-pocket costs for diagnostics
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Wide availability of generic antibiotics and expanding primary care services
